summary_one_line: Reaching get_feature_list requires a parseable GSUBGPOS table whose FourCC tag bytes GSUB/GPOS sit at offsets 0x0c-0x0f.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: An sfnt header whose table-directory record carries the literal 4-byte ASCII tag 'GSUB' (0x47 0x53 0x55 0x42) or 'GPOS' (0x47 0x50 0x4f 0x53) at offsets 0x0c-0x0f, so the OpenType table-tag dispatch routes the bytes into a GSUBGPOS structure and get_feature_list() (and the false/default arm at line 4253) is exercised.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: Every winner-resolving seed places the exact FourCC 'GPOS' or 'GSUB' run at offsets 0x0c-0x0f; the BYTE DIFF confirms those four offsets are saturated to the ASCII tag bytes for W (47(G)x20, 50(P)x16/53(S)x4, 4f(O)x16/55(U)x4, 53(S)x16/42(B)x4) while L holds none, satisfying the magic-tag comparison that gates table parsing.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: Loser-blocking seeds are short and contain no GSUB/GPOS tag at 0x0c-0x0f (bytes there are 00/01/20/2c), so the table-tag comparison never matches, the GSUBGPOS structure is never parsed, and the default arm at 4253 is only ever taken on the true side (L4253 T=236 F=0) without resolving the blocked false side.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: cmplog/vpc intercept the table-tag memcmp/integer comparison against the constant 'GSUB'/'GPOS', record both operands into the CmpLogObserver buffer, and I2SRandReplace substitutes the constant tag bytes into the input at the matching offsets; naive and value_profile lack I2SRandReplace and cannot synthesize the exact 4-byte tag by chance, so they stay blocked.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker is the default arm of a switch on the GSUBGPOS table version, reached only after the table is parsed.
Both decisive pairs differ by exactly the I2S technique.
The second decisive pair is value_profile_cmplog over value_profile, also an I2S delta.
Winner-resolving seeds carry the literal FourCC tag GPOS in the table directory.
Another winner-resolving seed carries the literal FourCC tag GSUB.
Offsets 0x0c-0x0f are saturated with the ASCII GSUB/GPOS tag bytes for W but differ entirely for L.
Loser-blocking seeds are short and lack the tag bytes, so they never resolve the blocked false side.
The loser never takes the false (default) side of the blocker while the winner does.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: d=1   L4253  T=80 F=62  T=236 F=0  default: return Null (FeatureList);  <-- BLOCKER

evidence_trail.claim: cmplog records both CMP operands and I2SRandReplace substitutes one operand at matching input offsets.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
`I2SRandReplace` reads the post-execution `CmpLogObserver` buffer,
picks a CMP entry, scans the input for byte sequences matching one
operand, and substitutes the other operand at those offsets.

evidence_trail.claim: value_profile lacks I2SRandReplace, so it cannot perform the operand substitution that cmplog/vpc can.

mechanism_consistency_check.verification_method: Ran `python3 tools/db_query.py lineage --branch 5547 --role W --fuzzer cmplog --trial 1 --seed 002c9ad92acce91b`; the 31-level ancestor chain contains I2S-floor dash rows (mutation_op = -) at depth 29 and 30, the floor signal for an unwrapped I2SRandReplace find under cmplog in the current build.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness gating on the 4-byte GSUB/GPOS tag at offset 0x0c in which naive/value_profile resolve the branch as often as cmplog/vpc, showing the exact tag is reachable without I2S substitution.

weakest_evidence_point: The exact input offset of the tag comparison (0x0c) is inferred from seed bytes and the sfnt table-directory layout rather than from an instrumented CMP-operand record, so a different alignment could shift the relevant offsets.
